element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Silent stealth weapon; allows non-alarm attacks on enemies
Standard ranged weapon; poison bolts enable stealth kills without alarm
Allows tactical approach; dagger and poisoned crossbow bolts avoid triggering alarms
Non-stealth weapon fire sets off alarms and triggers guard combat
Powerful combat weapon found throughout game world
Store first aid kits, armor, gold coins for later use
Quest assignment and plot progression; central gameplay mechanic
Central town from which player travels to missions; persistent level state
Improvable stat affecting ranged weapon accuracy
Plot device providing assistance and commentary throughout game
Sabotage missions weakening the Order; story progression
Main quest objective; powerful weapon requiring assembly
Provides critical plot information; player can choose to trust/betray
Kill Oracle or kill Macil; branches rest of story

typical_tension_triggers: Guard patrols and Order infantry combat
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with mission-based tension cycles; hub-world design allows player-controlled pacing between safe areas and combat zones

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Backtracking between hub and various areas; some areas remain persistent with player-discoverable secrets
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Stealth-accessible areas avoid alarm triggers; poison bolts and dagger enable alternative routes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Dagger-only start with basic stealth mechanics; introduction to alarm system and NPC interaction in Tarnhill hub; learning resource management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon variety expansion (crossbow, flamethrower); mission chains weakening Order; introduction of major combat encounters with infantry and war robots
difficulty_curve.late_game: Castle assault with multiple Order leaders; Sigil fragment acquisition battles; branching narrative choices with high consequence; final assembly of powerful artifact
notable_spikes: First major combat mission from Macil
Oracle/Macil choice point (narrative climax with mechanical consequence)
Castle assault (final mission with multiple boss-level encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: Tarnhill town serves as persistent safe hub; players travel outward to missions and return; areas retain their state between visits, enabling non-linear play and backtracking
Level design presents two approaches: silent dagger/poison bolt routes that preserve stealth, or noisy weapon fire triggering guard alarms; creates tactical replayability
NPC quest assignment drives progression; each mission advances plot and weakens Order; creates narrative pacing separate from spatial progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple areas assigned by Macil and Blackbird

notable_level_patterns.description: Defeating major Order leaders (Programmer, Bishop) yields Sigil fragments; creates hierarchical difficulty scaling and narrative significance for major encounters
Oracle/Macil decision point (mid-game) branches story into two paths; creates mechanical divergence in level geography and quest availability
